paragraphs.text:
SRD-46 does **not contain direct stability-constant entries** for **DMF, acetonitrile, THF, or ethylene glycol** with **Fe²⁺ (metal_62)** or **Fe³⁺ (metal_61)**, and a solvent-name scan for measurements in **DMF / MeCN / THF / ethylene glycol media** also returned **no hits**.

So the best SRD-46-backed answer is: **no direct database constants**, plus a **reasoned estimate** from the closest weak-donor Fe systems that do exist. The analog coverage is imperfect: the database mostly contains **anionic O-donor ligands** or **multidentate amide-carboxylates**, which are all stronger than the neutral solvents you asked about.

paragraphs.text:
## 2) Closest analogs actually found for Fe²⁺ / Fe³⁺

These are **not direct substitutes**, but they bracket the chemistry.

| Metal | Analog ligand in SRD-46 | Donor character | T (°C) | I (M) | Comment |
|---|---|---|---:|---:|---|
| Fe²⁺ | Hydroxyacetic acid (glycolic acid), ligand_8640 | O-donor, anionic hydroxy-carboxylate | 25 | 1 | Much stronger than neutral alcohol/diol donors |
| Fe²⁺ | Oxydiacetic acid, ligand_8974 | O,O,O donor, dianionic ether-dicarboxylate | 25 | 0.1 | Much stronger than THF/ether |
| Fe²⁺ | Amide-containing polyaminocarboxylates, ligand_6050/6051/6052/6053 | amide + carboxylate multidentate | 37 | 0.15 | Not good quantitative analogs; too strong |
| Fe³⁺ | Hydroxyacetic acid (glycolic acid), ligand_8640 | O-donor, anionic hydroxy-carboxylate | 25 | 1 | Stronger than neutral EG by charge assistance |
| Fe³⁺ | Oxydiacetic acid, ligand_8974 | O,O,O donor, dianionic | 25 | 0.5 | Far stronger than THF |
| Fe³⁺ | Salicylamide-type ligands, ligand_9320/9322 | phenolate/amide systems | 25 | 3 | Too strong, not direct solvent analogs |

paragraphs.text:
## 3) Reasoned qualitative ranking

For **neutral ligands in water**, expected intrinsic binding to iron should follow donor hardness/chelation:

| Metal | Expected order among your targets |
|---|---|
| Fe²⁺ | ethylene glycol > DMF ≳ THF > acetonitrile |
| Fe³⁺ | ethylene glycol > DMF > THF > acetonitrile |

Why:
- **Ethylene glycol** can act as a **neutral O,O-chelate**, giving an entropic advantage over monodentate donors.
- **DMF** is a decent **carbonyl O donor**.
- **THF** is a weaker neutral ether O donor.
- **Acetonitrile** is usually a relatively weak ligand for hard aqueous Fe ions, especially versus water/hydrolysis competition.
- For **Fe³⁺**, hydrolysis competes so strongly in water that weak neutral ligands are often effectively suppressed unless the medium is strongly acidic or nonaqueous.

claims.text: ## 3) Reasoned qualitative ranking
For **neutral ligands in water**, expected intrinsic binding to iron should follow donor hardness/chelation:
| Fe²⁺ | ethylene glycol > DMF ≳ THF > acetonitrile |
| Fe³⁺ | ethylene glycol > DMF > THF > acetonitrile |
Why:
**Ethylene glycol** can act as a **neutral O,O-chelate**
giving an entropic advantage over monodentate donors.
**DMF** is a decent **carbonyl O donor**.
**THF** is a weaker neutral ether O donor.
**Acetonitrile** is usually a relatively weak ligand for hard aqueous Fe ions
especially versus water/hydrolysis competition.
For **Fe³⁺**, hydrolysis competes so strongly in water
weak neutral ligands are often effectively suppressed unless the medium is strongly acidic or nonaqueous.

grounded_claims.evidence_snippet: Filler span: no useful database-groundable information.
Ethylene glycol (HO–CH2–CH2–OH) is a neutral diol that can bind as an O,O-chelate; SRD-46 provides no specific Fe–ethylene glycol rows here.
Chelate effect: multidentate binding has an entropic advantage over monodentate donors; not a database row-level fact.
DMF donates via carbonyl O and is a moderate neutral donor; SRD-46 has no Fe–DMF aqueous stability entries here.
THF is an ether O donor and typically weaker than carbonyl O donors; no SRD-46 rows here for Fe–THF in water.
Acetonitrile is generally a weak ligand for hard aqueous Fe ions; SRD-46 has no confirming rows here.
Fe3+ forms many hydroxo species in water, indicating strong hydrolysis that outcompetes weak neutral donors.

paragraphs.text:
## 4) Estimated aqueous log β1 values from SRD-46 analog trends

These are **reasoned estimates**, not database measurements. They should be treated as **order-of-magnitude guidance** only.

| System | Estimated log β1 | Confidence | Rationale |
|---|---:|---|---|
| Fe²⁺–DMF | ~0 to 1 | Low | Neutral amide O donor; weaker than any carboxylate-containing analog in SRD-46 |
| Fe³⁺–DMF | ~0 to 2 | Low | Harder cation favors O donor, but hydrolysis strongly limits observable aqueous binding |
| Fe²⁺–MeCN | ≤0.5 | Low | Weak neutral nitrile donor; likely very small in water |
| Fe³⁺–MeCN | ~0 to 1 | Very low | Could bind in nonaqueous media, but aqueous hydrolysis likely dominates |
| Fe²⁺–THF | ~0 to 0.5 | Low | Simple ether donor; weaker than carbonyl O donors |
| Fe³⁺–THF | ~0 to 1 | Very low | Hard-acid/ether interaction possible, but water competition severe |
| Fe²⁺–ethylene glycol | ~0.5 to 1.5 | Low–moderate | Neutral O,O chelation should make it the strongest of the four |
| Fe³⁺–ethylene glycol | ~1 to 3 | Low | Fe³⁺ prefers O donors, but free hydrolysis still competes strongly in water |

paragraphs.text:
## 5) Practical interpretation

| Target | Likely aqueous behavior with Fe²⁺ | Likely aqueous behavior with Fe³⁺ |
|---|---|---|
| DMF | Weak, probably only minor inner-sphere association | Weak to modest only under strongly controlled acidic conditions |
| Acetonitrile | Very weak | Very weak in water; more relevant in nonaqueous coordination chemistry |
| THF | Very weak | Very weak |
| Ethylene glycol | Weak but potentially detectable as chelate | Strongest of the set, but still far below anionic O-donor ligands |

paragraphs.text:
## Bottom line

| Question | Answer |
|---|---|
| Are direct SRD-46 constants available for DMF–Fe(II)/Fe(III), MeCN–Fe(II)/Fe(III), THF–Fe(II)/Fe(III), ethylene glycol–Fe(II)/Fe(III)? | **No direct entries found** |
| Best-supported qualitative strength order | **ethylene glycol > DMF ≳ THF > acetonitrile** |
| Expected magnitude | **all weak in water**, especially for Fe³⁺ because of **hydrolysis competition** |
| Best rough estimate range | **log β1 mostly 0–1 for Fe²⁺ neutral monodentates; up to ~1–3 for Fe³⁺ with ethylene glycol under favorable conditions** |

If you want, I can do one more pass focused specifically on **Fe²⁺ weak O-donor analogs** in SRD-46 and build a tighter comparison table of actual measured analog constants and conditions.
